title: Node Process, Event Loop, Modules, Configuration, and Lifecycle
description: The service reads configuration during requests, starts background promises without ownership, mixes module systems, treats signals as ordinary exceptions, and reports ready before dependencies exist. Learners cumulatively produce process, event-loop, configuration, and lifecycle state model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace call stack, timers, pending callbacks, poll, check, close callbacks, microtasks, and next-tick work without assuming one request owns one event-loop turn.
Configure ESM, CommonJS interop, package exports, file extensions, and NodeNext resolution without relying on accidental development-loader behavior.
Parse, validate, normalize, redact, and freeze configuration once before acquiring resources or accepting traffic.
